summary: Small single-story rectangular Minecraft building sitting inside a shallow recessed foundation. Walls are mainly horizontal wooden planks with vertical log corner posts, a continuous band of stonebrick (and slab) near the top, a dark brick (netherbrick) trim band above that, and a flat raised roof with an inner recessed netherbrick floor. Windows are a row of glass panes on the visible long side. A low stonebrick border surrounds the building at ground level.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with recessed inner roof area and a surrounding low stone border
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along both length and width (appears centered)

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: base border, trim band and slabs

materials.name: netherbrick
materials.role: upper trim band and recessed roof floor

rebuild_hints: Create a rectangular wooden-walled box (approx 9x13) one story tall with vertical logs at corners and every ~4 blocks along the wall where visible.
Add a continuous stonebrick layer (and stone slabs as edge) around the top of the wooden walls, then a single horizontal row of netherbrick above that.
Leave the central roof area recessed and fill it with netherbrick; cap the outer roof rim with stone slabs to make a flat framed roof.
Surround the building with a one-block-high stonebrick border platform at ground level matching the pictures.
uncertainties: Exact block counts for width/depth/height are estimated from perspective images; actual footprint may differ by a few blocks.
Door location(s) not visible in provided views; entrance orientation is unknown.
There may be additional detailing on hidden sides (back) not visible in the images.
The ground surface around the stone border appears coarse (dirt/gravel/path) but exact material is uncertain.
